page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7a-nps1.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance belonging to the ecs.c7a family. It features 32 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7T83 processor running at 2.55 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25% at 2697.59. Multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall within the average range. Cryptographic capabilities are notable, with PassMark CPU encryption tests scoring in the top 25% at 34051.87, supported by hardware-accelerated AES and VAES instruction sets. Memory performance is also strong, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top 25%, while large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidths remain average.

Given its compute-heavy profile and lack of bundled local storage, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated, non-shared CPU execution without massive memory footprints. It is well-suited for high-throughput web serving, multi-threaded decompression tasks, cryptography-heavy applications, and distributed compute workloads.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7a-nps1.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC 7T83 physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, operating at 2.55 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, it delivers an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks highlight strong single-core CPU execution and encryption capabilities, both ranking in the top 25%, alongside average multi-core performance. This instance is designed for compute-intensive workloads such as cryptography, multi-threaded decompression, and high-throughput web serving where dedicated CPU resources are required.
